questions.question_text: In Iowa, is a real estate license required to manage property for compensation?
distractors: Only if managing 10 or more units
questions.explanation: Iowa requires a real estate license to manage property for compensation. Property managers must operate under a licensed broker and maintain written property management agreements specifying scope, compensation, duration, and responsibilities.

questions.question_text: Under HF 2326 (effective April 2024), which activity can an unlicensed broker employee perform for residential rentals?
distractors: Preparing purchase agreements for home sales
questions.explanation: HF 2326 (April 2024, codified at Iowa Code 543B.7A) allows unlicensed employees of a broker to advertise, show property, list, collect rents and deposits, procure prospects, and complete and execute form agreements for residential rentals under chapters 562A and 562B. Negotiating lease terms and any sales-side activities remain licensed activities.

questions.question_text: A property manager collects rent and security deposits from tenants. Where must these funds be held?
distractors: In the property owner's personal account
questions.explanation: Iowa requires property managers to maintain trust accounts for owner and tenant funds, including rent payments and security deposits. Commingling these funds with personal or business operating accounts is a violation of Iowa real estate law.

questions.question_text: Which element is required in an Iowa property management agreement?
distractors: Only the monthly management fee amount
Only the property street address listed
Only the property owner's legal signature
questions.explanation: Iowa requires written property management agreements that include the scope of services, compensation structure, duration of the agreement, and responsibilities of both parties. This protects both the property owner and the manager.

questions.question_text: A newly hired unlicensed office assistant at a brokerage wants to negotiate lease renewal terms with tenants. Under HF 2326, is this permitted?
distractors: Yes, if the broker approves in writing
questions.explanation: HF 2326 allows unlicensed broker employees to handle advertising, showings, rent collection, and form completion for residential rentals. However, negotiating lease terms remains a licensed activity that requires a real estate license.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum security deposit a landlord can collect in Iowa?
questions.explanation: Iowa law limits security deposits to a maximum of two months' rent. This applies to all residential rental properties. The deposit must be held and returned according to the requirements of Iowa Code Chapter 562A (URLTA).

questions.question_text: A tenant moves out of an Iowa rental on March 1. By what date must the landlord return the security deposit?
questions.explanation: Iowa requires landlords to return the security deposit, or furnish a written statement of the specific reasons for withholding it, within 30 days of the termination of the tenancy and receipt of the tenant's mailing address or delivery instructions (Iowa Code 562A.12(3)). Assuming the tenant provided a forwarding address at the March 1 move-out, the deadline is March 31.

questions.question_text: A tenant fails to pay rent on the due date. What is the minimum notice period for an Iowa eviction based on nonpayment?
questions.explanation: Iowa requires a 3-day notice for eviction based on nonpayment of rent. Other notice periods include 7 days for lease violations and 30 days for termination of a month-to-month tenancy. The tenant may cure by paying within the notice period.

questions.question_text: A tenant reports a code violation to the health department. Two weeks later, the landlord serves a 30-day termination notice. What defense does the tenant have?
questions.correct_answer: A one-year presumption of retaliation applies
distractors: No defense if the notice is properly served
The tenant must prove the landlord's malice
Retaliation claims expire after only 30 days
questions.explanation: Iowa Code 562A.36 prohibits retaliatory conduct, including bringing an action for possession because the tenant complained to a code-enforcement agency. Evidence of a good-faith complaint within one year before the alleged retaliation creates a rebuttable presumption that the landlord acted in retaliation, which the tenant can raise as a defense in court. The presumption shifts the burden to the landlord; it does not apply if the tenant is in default in rent.

questions.question_text: An Iowa landlord deducts carpet cleaning from the security deposit but provides no documentation. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: Landlord must provide an itemized statement
distractors: The deduction is automatically deemed valid
The tenant forfeits the full security deposit
The landlord may keep the entire deposit amount
questions.explanation: Iowa law requires landlords to provide an itemized statement of deductions when returning a security deposit. Failure to provide this statement within 30 days may result in the landlord forfeiting the right to claim deductions and owing the full deposit.

questions.question_text: Which uniform act governs landlord-tenant relations in Iowa?
questions.explanation: Iowa adopted the Uniform Residential Landlord and Tenant Act (URLTA), codified as Chapter 562A. This act establishes the rights and obligations of landlords and tenants in residential rental agreements throughout the state.

questions.question_text: Under Iowa's URLTA, what obligation does a landlord have regarding the condition of rental property?
questions.correct_answer: Maintain the property in habitable condition
distractors: Only repair what the lease specifies
Repair only if tenant pays for materials
questions.explanation: Iowa's URLTA imposes a habitability obligation on landlords, requiring them to maintain rental properties in a condition fit for human habitation. This includes maintaining structural elements, plumbing, heating, and electrical systems.

questions.question_text: A city council proposes an ordinance capping monthly rent increases at 3%. Is this enforceable in Iowa?
questions.correct_answer: No, Iowa does not allow rent control
distractors: Yes, cities may set local rent limits
Yes, if approved by the state legislature
questions.explanation: Iowa does not permit rent control at any level of government. Local municipalities cannot enact ordinances that cap or limit rent increases. Landlords may set and adjust rents according to market conditions and lease terms.

questions.question_text: When does Iowa law require a written lease for a residential tenancy?
questions.correct_answer: When the lease term exceeds one year
distractors: For all residential tenancies regardless of term
Only for fully furnished apartment rentals
Only when the security deposit exceeds $500
questions.explanation: Under Iowa's statute of frauds (Iowa Code 622.32(3)), contracts creating an interest in land must be in writing, except leases for a term not exceeding one year. A residential lease longer than one year is unenforceable unless in writing; leases of one year or less, including month-to-month tenancies, may be oral, though written leases are recommended for clarity.

questions.question_text: Under URLTA, what information must a landlord provide to the tenant at the start of a tenancy?
questions.correct_answer: Name and address of landlord or PM
distractors: The property's current assessed tax value
The names and contact info of all neighbors
questions.explanation: Iowa's URLTA requires landlords to notify tenants of the name and address of the landlord or property manager responsible for the premises. This ensures tenants know who to contact for maintenance requests, notices, and other communications.
